mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically elevating equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which directly suppresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer spending strength across durable and non-durable sectors, driving higher corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 55% of cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which triggers immediate positive market repricing of near-term earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during inflationary supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 2.5 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent, markets price in imminent monetary tightening and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered observations over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent signals severe aggregate demand contraction, which forces equity markets to downgrade earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in > 50% of low-growth quarters despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between payroll growth and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.04 across all tested sub-periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month expansions in industrial production and retail sales confirm synchronized supply and demand growth, eliminating inventory buildup risks and triggering immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive returns in > 60% of synchronized expansion months over a 4-year rolling window.
